mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression or positive return frequency falls below 45% when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero during expansion periods, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent annually signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings pressure, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in > 60% of sub-1% growth quarters or rolling Rank IC remains negative over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels surpassing 220.0 indicate accelerating inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances above the threshold or predictive accuracy drops below random chance over 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over 5-year rolling windows or payroll growth shows no significant correlation with 20-day forward returns in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production growth outpaces retail sales growth indicates building inventory buffers and supply-side resilience, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant or positive return frequency falls below 40% when the divergence condition holds over 3-year windows.
